name: bastra__save_memory
description:
[Bastra-recall (persistent memory)] Persist a new memory into the vault as a markdown file with YAML frontmatter. This is YOUR long-term memory — save autonomously when a memory-worthy moment occurs, do not wait to be asked.

STRONG SIGNALS — save without confirmation, then 1-line ack:
- User expresses repetition/frustration about a recurring issue   ('wieder', 'schon wieder', 'wie oft', emphatic caps) → lesson
- User states an explicit durable rule ('immer X', 'nie Y', 'bei   diesem Projekt nutzen wir Z') → preference / workflow
- User corrects a recurring tendency in your behavior →   meta-working
- An architectural decision is finalized after weighing options   → decision
- User confirms a workflow ('lass uns das immer so machen') →   workflow
- A bug got fixed after >2 iterations with non-obvious root   cause → lesson (capture the FAILED PATH too, not just the fix)

ANTI-SIGNALS — do NOT save:
- One-off task descriptions ('baue mir bitte X') — that's a   task, not a memory
- Speculation, 'maybe' statements, tentative ideas
- Anything derivable from code/git/CLAUDE.md
- Sensitive personal data (unless a stable preference)
- When unsure: default to NOT saving. False saves erode trust.

BEFORE SAVING: call recall() with the title/topic to check for an existing memory you should update (overwrite=true) instead of creating a duplicate.

QUALITY BARS:
- Title: short, specific, non-generic.
- Summary (<=400 chars): one sentence with the gist.
- Body: lead with the rule/fact, then **Why:** (root cause /   reason / incident) and **How to apply:** (when this kicks in).   For lessons, capture the failure path AND the fix.
- recall_when (CRITICAL — highest-weighted search field): 2-4   CONCRETE contexts/queries where future-you should be reminded.   'about to write a Tailwind grid' beats 'CSS questions'. Without   good recall_when, the memory is dead weight.

AFTER SAVING: surface a single-line ack to the user, prefixed with `→`: `→ saved: <title> (id: <id>)`. Nothing more.

type.description: Memory type. Use 'lesson' for fixes/gotchas, 'preference' for project-scoped style choices, 'user-preference' for the human's cross-project preferences, 'project-fact' for non-derivable project state, 'decision' for committed design decisions, 'workflow' for recurring procedures.

summary.description: One sentence (<=400 chars) capturing the gist — appears in recall() hits.

body.description: Full markdown body. Lead with the rule/fact, then explain *why* (the reason/incident) and *how to apply* (when this kicks in). Wikilinks like [[other-memory-id]] are supported.

tags.description: Flat tags for filtering, at least one.

scope.description: Project/area this memory belongs to, e.g. 'bastra-recall', 'carnexus', 'user-preference', 'all-projects'.

recall_when.description: Trigger phrases — situations where this memory should surface. Highest-weighted search field. Be specific: 'about to write a Tailwind grid', not 'CSS questions'.

related.description: Optional ids of related memories. Hinweis: `[[id]]`-Wikilinks im body werden automatisch ins related[] gespiegelt — du musst sie hier nicht doppelt aufzählen.

sensitivity.description: Wer darf das Memory sehen? Default 'team' (lokale KI-Tools). 'private' = nur Mac-App (für externe Caller nicht sichtbar).

expires_after_days.description: Tage nach 'updated', ab denen das Memory altert/expires. Überschreibt den Type-Default (lesson=180, decision=365, …).

last_reviewed_at.description: ISO-Datum des letzten 'noch aktuell'-Checks. Resetet Staleness.

affects_files.description: Optionale Liste von Repo-Pfaden, die diese Lesson/Decision betrifft.

confidence.description: 0-1, default 1. Lower if the lesson is tentative.

id.description: Optional explicit id/slug. Default: slugified title.

overwrite.description: If true, replace an existing memory with the same id. Default false (errors on collision).
